description: Fix status handling so timeouts/rate-limits never produce 'blocked' status, and auth-invalid always produces 'disabled' status. Three locations need fixing: (1) getConnectionAuthBlockedPatch in usageStatusPatches.ts always returns 'disabled' when authState is invalid, (2) markAccountUnavailable fallback in auth.tsx never sets 'blocked' for transient timeout/rate-limit errors, (3) connectionUsageRefresh.ts catch-all fallback for 429 errors does not set routingStatus to 'blocked'.
steps: In src/lib/usageStatusPatches.ts: Change getConnectionAuthBlockedPatch() to ALWAYS return routingStatus:'disabled' and reasonCode:'auth_invalid' regardless of whether the error message matches the narrower requiresReauthorization patterns. When authState is 'invalid', the token needs manual intervention regardless of the specific reason. Remove the requiresReauthorization conditional - always use 'disabled' and 'auth_invalid'.

In src/sse/services/auth.tsx: In the markAccountUnavailable fallback block (the `if (!canonicalBlockedPatch && !kiroRetestValid)` section around line 860), change the condition to also treat HTTP 429, 502, and 504 status codes as transient (just like isProviderTransientProcessingError). These should keep routingStatus 'eligible' with reasonCode 'transient_upstream_error' instead of falling through to 'blocked'. The condition should be: `isProviderTransientProcessingError || isTransientFallbackStatus` where isTransientFallbackStatus is true when status is 429, 502, or 504. This way the fallback block only sets 'blocked' for genuinely non-transient, non-timeout, non-rate-limit errors.

In src/lib/connectionUsageRefresh.ts: In the catch-all error handler (around line 880-900), the fallback block that currently sets routingStatus:'blocked' when status is NOT 429 should also NOT set blocked for 502/504 (timeout indicators). For 429, the existing code already avoids blocked (it sets nextRetryAt only). For 502 and 504, the fallback should keep the current routingStatus (or 'eligible') and set healthStatus:'degraded' with reasonCode:'transient_upstream_error' instead of 'blocked'. This is the section: `authBlockedPatch || { routingStatus: 'blocked', ... }`.

Add or update tests in tests/unit/ to verify: (A) getConnectionAuthBlockedPatch returns 'disabled' for all auth errors (not just reauthorization patterns), (B) markAccountUnavailable does NOT produce 'blocked' for 429/502/504 errors when no canonical patch matches, (C) connectionUsageRefresh does not set 'blocked' for 502/504 transient errors.
acceptance_criteria: markAccountUnavailable with status 429 never produces routingStatus:'blocked' in the final connection patch
markAccountUnavailable with status 502 (non-specific error text) never produces routingStatus:'blocked'
markAccountUnavailable with status 504 never produces routingStatus:'blocked'
connectionUsageRefresh catch-all for 502/504 does not set routingStatus:'blocked'
